paper_title: An optimised method for intact nuclei isolation from diatoms

methods.description: Process for growing algal cultures and preparing samples for further analysis.

steps.description: Pseudo-nitzschia multistriata, Phaeodactylum tricornutum, and Chaetoceros diadema wild type cells were grown in f/2 Guillard medium in 12L/12D cycles at 18 °C under white fluorescence neon lamps at irradiance of 60 μmol m−2 s−1.
steps.searchable_summary: grow algal cultures in specified conditions

steps.description: For each experiment, 100 mL of cell culture were collected during the exponential growth phase at specified cell densities.
steps.searchable_summary: collect cell culture samples at specified densities

steps.description: Cells were collected by centrifugation at 1800 ×g in 50 mL conical tubes using a swinging bucket angle rotor refrigerated centrifuge at 18 °C.
inputs: swinging bucket angle rotor refrigerated centrifuge

steps.description: Cell pellets were transferred to 2 mL Eppendorf tubes, washed with 2 mL of ASW, centrifuged 10′ at 1500 ×g in a refrigerated microcentrifuge at 18 °C, and incubated for 10′ in 400 µL of NH4F (10 M, pH 5) at room temperature.
steps.searchable_summary: wash and incubate cell pellets with NH4F

methods.description: Procedure for sonication of NH4F treated cells to isolate nuclei.

steps.description: NH4F treated cells were washed with 2 mL of ASW and then resuspended in 200 µL of cold Nuclei Isolation Buffer (NIB) in 1.5 mL Eppendorf tubes.
steps.searchable_summary: wash and resuspend cells in NIB

steps.description: Sonication was performed using a Branson 450 digital sonifier with a 20 kHz 102-C converter. The 1.5 mL tube was positioned in a small rack on ice and the probe of the sonicator was immersed in the sample at the center of the tube.
steps.searchable_summary: sonicate cells using Branson 450 digital sonifier

steps.description: Depending on the species, 5 to 20 repetitions of 15′′ pulses (40 W intensity) were applied with 10′′ breaks in between.
